perceptual_narrative_logs.visual_narrative_log: (1) The video begins with a 2D animated character resembling Namewee sitting in a chair against a simple wooden wall, introducing the story. (2) It transitions to a flashback in an animated classroom where a gray-haired male teacher stands behind a donation box. (3) Various students are shown, including one mentioning 'BMW' and 'Toyota'. (4) The main Namewee character is shown at his desk, looking frustrated, with a small blue demon appearing on his shoulder to advise him. (5) He then stands up and rallies his classmates in front of a chalkboard. (6) The scene shifts to the teacher confronting Namewee, and then to the principal's office where the principal (a bald man in a striped shirt) scolds the teacher. (7) The animation returns to the narrator in the initial room. (8) At 01:26, the medium shifts to live-action, showing adults in a chaotic indoor playground/fast-food setting, some dressed in mascot-like costumes (red wigs, boots), interacting aggressively while a man plays a ukulele.
perceptual_narrative_logs.audio_narrative_log: (1) A distinctive, high-pitched male voice (Namewee) introduces himself over upbeat, bouncy background music. (2) A more serious, slightly deeper male voice (the teacher) speaks, followed by various student voices responding with car brand names. (3) Transition SFX (wooshes and pings) accompany the character's thoughts and the appearance of the blue demon. (4) The audio features a mix of dialogue and dramatic musical stings during the confrontation. (5) In the final segment, the audio changes to a full musical track: an upbeat folk-pop song featuring ukulele and Namewee's singing voice, overlaid with ambient noise of people shouting and crashing sounds in a live environment.
perceptual_narrative_logs.text_narrative_log: (1) Functional Text: Traditional Chinese subtitles translate all spoken dialogue throughout the video. (2) Decorative/Hook Text: Large yellow and white text appears for emphasis, such as 'Namewee', 'BMW', 'Toyota', '1000', '500', and '100'. (3) Large red/yellow captions like 'This is robbery!' (搶劫) and 'Justice Fairy' (正義仙子) appear for comedic effect. (4) Promotional Text: '黃明志最新專輯《小明與小志》正式上線!' (Namewee's latest album 'Xiao Ming and Xiao Zhi' is officially online!) appears during the live-action transition.

temporal_pacing_summary.mainActionFlow: Intro -> Flashback Story (Conflict/Resolution) -> Conclusion -> Live-action Music Promotion

visual_human_presence.countingEvidence: 1 main animated creator, 1 animated teacher, 1 animated principal, multiple animated students; 5+ live-action adults and 1 child in the final segment.
visual_human_presence.mainCharacterDescription: An animated version of Namewee wearing a black beanie, black t-shirt, and glasses; in the live-action segment, he wears a striped shirt with a heart patch and a cat-ear beanie.

visual_scene_and_style.backgroundDescribe: A simple room with wood-paneled walls; a classroom with a chalkboard and lockers; a principal's office; an indoor children's play area with posters and colorful furniture.
visual_scene_and_style.lightingStyle: Bright, even studio lighting in both animated and live-action scenes.

audio_production_style.audioTransition: Hard cut between story and song; SFX transitions between animated scenes.

vocal_qualities.pitch: Variable (high for Namewee, deep for Teacher)

narrative_and_purpose.narrativeStructure: Linear: Hook -> Conflict -> Resolution -> Promotional CTA
narrative_and_purpose.mainPurpose: The video uses a humorous animated anecdote about social class discrimination in schools to engage the audience before transitioning into a live-action music video segment promoting Namewee's new album 'Xiao Ming and Xiao Zhi'. The story serves as a thematic bridge to the album's content.

endorsement_analysis.endorsementCues: Direct textual announcement and visual promotion of the album release.

social_sociology_tags.uniqueSignatures: Namewee's signature animated avatar and distinctive vocal style.
social_sociology_tags.freeDescribe: The video satirizes educational systems and wealth-based biases, characteristic of the creator's social-commentary style.
